title: Pinball Design Knowledge Base
summary: Structured design patterns and implementation rules for physical, virtual, and fantasy pinball games, optimized for game development using the Flame Engine.

components.name: Flipper
components.behavior: Solenoid-driven lever rotatable up to a physical limit stop. Returns via spring tension.
components.player_impact: Primary control mechanism. Used for aiming, trapping (cradling), and passing.

implementation.control_mechanism: High instantaneous torque application on press; spring-back torque on release.

components.name: Ramp
components.behavior: Elevated 3D pathways that redirect the ball vertically and horizontally.
components.player_impact: Maintains velocity, redirects ball back to opposite flipper, builds kinetic rhythm (flow).

implementation.z_ordering: Rendered on higher layer; collision mask active only when ball ascends entry ramp.

components.name: Orbit / Loop
components.behavior: Outer boundary lanes looping the ball back to the upper playfield or flippers.
components.player_impact: Sustains maximum speed and velocity loops.

components.name: Pop Bumper
components.behavior: Solenoid-activated circular skirt that fires outward with force upon ball contact.
components.player_impact: Injects velocity and structural chaos, breaking deterministic paths.

implementation.collision_listener: Detect contact, apply radial impulse vector to ball, play sound, trigger scale pulse animation.

components.name: Slingshot
components.behavior: Triangular kicker bands above the flippers activated by contact switches.
components.player_impact: Pushes ball horizontally across flippers, threatening outlane drains.

implementation.collision_listener: Apply normal impulse vector away from slingshot surface on contact.

components.name: Drop Target
components.behavior: Vertical target cards that fall flat or drop into the playfield when hit.
components.player_impact: Clear goals for ruleset progression, mode activation, and score multipliers.

implementation.collision_listener: Disable collision body upon hit, run retraction animation, update table state.

components.name: Bash Toy
components.behavior: Interactive mechanical targets (e.g. moving bosses, opening chests) with custom animations.
components.player_impact: Focal point of table themes; yields high rewards and satisfying kinetic feedback.

implementation.collision_listener: Track hit counts, play damage animations, toggle path gates.

techniques.name: Cradling
techniques.description: Catching and holding the ball on an active flipper to stop momentum and set up shots.
techniques.physics_involved: Frictional deceleration, gravity balance, flipper resting angle.

techniques.name: Dead Flipping
techniques.description: Allowing a high-speed ball to bounce off an inactive flipper to absorb kinetic energy and roll to the opposite side.
techniques.physics_involved: Restitution coefficient, material damping of flipper rubber.

techniques.name: Post-Passing
techniques.description: Bouncing the ball from the tip of one raised flipper to the base of the other flipper.
techniques.physics_involved: Angular momentum transfer, precise collision angle.

techniques.name: Nudging
techniques.description: Shaking or shifting the cabinet to alter the ball's trajectory, avoiding the outlanes.
techniques.physics_involved: Momentum transfer to dynamic body relative to static boundary frames.

rulesets.modes: Time-limited shot targets representing missions or stories.
rulesets.multiball: 2-6 balls active concurrently with multiplied scoring structures.
rulesets.stacking: Concurrent activation of modes, multipliers, and multiballs for optimal points.
rulesets.wizard_mode: Ultimate state unlocked after beating all primary modes.

fantasy_innovations.description: Dynamic enemies moving across the field, changing layouts, and destructible geometry.
Air control (nudging/steering in mid-air), localized anti-gravity fields, magnetic traps, and speed portals.
Purchasable table modifications (upgraded flippers, ball shields), character health bars, and boss battles.

physics.revolute_joint_torque: Apply high impulse torque to simulate instant solenoid power.
physics.collision_filtering: Strict category bits to isolate ball, flippers, table walls, and sensors.

inputs.callbacks: TapCallbacks on full-screen input zones or KeyboardEvents for responsive flipper triggering.
inputs.latency_target: Sub-50ms reaction times to prevent control mismatch.
inputs.haptics: Light impacts for bumpers/slingshots, medium impacts for flipper hit limits.

camera.fixed_aspect: Vertical 9:16 layout locked inside a portrait viewport or center-letterboxed on landscape.
camera.smooth_tracking: Camera viewfinder follows the ball's Y-coordinate with a spring-damper interpolation.

juice.visuals: Pop bumpers pulse in scale, spark particle systems on metal collisions, score callouts floating upwards.
juice.time_dilation: Brief slow-motion trigger when ball is extremely close to outlane drain to allow recovery.
